sources.label: Oshkosh Defense ROGUE-Fires Block 2 award press release (June 1, 2026 - predates window)
WBAY: $92M USMC ROGUE-Fires delivery order, June 1, 2026
Stockanalysis.com OSK forecast page (consensus Buy, avg PT ~$166)
Daily Political: quarterly dividend $0.57 payable June 9, 2026 (routine)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and the stock is flat today (-0.03%) and YTD (-0.66%), so residual asymmetry on this latent-node screen is unchanged; the June 1 ROGUE-Fires Block 2 autonomy award predates the window and was a small ($92M) order already in the public domain before yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: The $92M ROGUE-Fires Block 2 order is the first large-scale production program for autonomous ground vehicles, directly validating the 'autonomous specialty platforms' latent-AI pathway, with deliveries through 2031 and an FMTV A2 order stack ($142M) behind it.
verdicts.neutral: OSK is a $8.1B diversified specialty-vehicle maker whose AI linkage is second-order; Q1 EPS fell to $0.68 from $1.72 but FY guidance of $10.90 was reaffirmed, and a flat tape leaves the entry point neither stretched nor distressed.
verdicts.bear: Access/Vocational cyclicality (construction, rental fleets) dominates earnings; tariff and mix pressure crushed Q1, and the autonomy orders are too small (~1% of revenue) to move the needle if the core cycle rolls over.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Follow-on autonomous ground vehicle awards (ROGUE-Fires Block 2 ramp, USPS NGDV/defense autonomy pipeline) or evidence of data-center construction demand flowing into access equipment orders.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Non-residential construction and rental capex downturn compressing Access segment margins, with back-end-weighted 2026 guidance ($10.90 EPS) at risk after the weak Q1.
